title: STM32 FreeRTOS LED Scheduler
connections: ST-Link SWDIO/SWCLK/GND/3.3V connected to STM32 debug port
tips: Use FreeRTOS API `vTaskDelay()` instead of HAL block delays. `vTaskDelay` relinquishes control, letting other lower-priority threads run.
Check your FreeRTOS config file: ensure system tick frequencies are set to 1000Hz (1ms resolution).

title: STM32 Multi-Channel DMA ADC Logger
connections: Sensors Analog Out → PA0, PA1, PA2
SD Card SPI: SCK → PA5, MISO → PA6, MOSI → PA7, CS → PA4
tips: The ADC peripheral updates conversion memory directly in RAM using DMA. Zero CPU Cycles are wasted.
Place decoupling capacitors near the STM32 analog power pins (VDDA/VSSA) to decouple switching loop noise.

title: STM32 parallel 16x2 LCD Driver
connections: LCD D4-D7 → PA2, PA3, PA4, PA5
LCD VSS/VCC → GND/5V, Contrast Middle Pin → V0
tips: Standard HD44780 LCD controller timing is slow. Inserting microscopic delays (microsecond delays) between pin writes is required for correct rendering.
The LCD logic requires 5V. Fortunately, most STM32 GPIO pins are 5V-tolerant (FT) in digital output modes.

title: STM32 CAN Bus Communication node
tips: A CAN bus MUST be terminated at each end with a 120Ω resistor. Without them, transmission frames fail due to signal reflections.
Check timing segments (TQ) carefully to avoid clock drift packet errors between nodes.

title: STM32 UART Interrupt Terminal Node
tips: Avoid writing print statements inside the ISR. Keep execution code inside `HAL_UART_RxCpltCallback` short to avoid framing errors.
Mismatching parity, baud rate (e.g. 115200), or stop bits will corrupt received characters.

title: STM32 Encoder Motor Controller
connections: Encoder Channel A/B → PA0/PA1 (TIM2 CH1/CH2 Input)
Motor Driver IN1/IN2 → PA6/PA7, PWM ENA → PA8 (TIM1 CH1 Output)
tips: STM32 Timer Encoder Mode handles reading quadrature pulses and tracking directions automatically in hardware, freeing up CPU cycles.
Encoder wires should be shielded or twisted to protect signals from motor line noise coupling.

title: STM32 multi-Timer PWM Servo Controller
connections: Servo Signals 1-4 → PA0, PA1, PA2, PA3 (TIM2 CH1-CH4)
VCC → 5V External (+), GND → STM32 GND & 5V External (-)
tips: Timer calculations: with a 72MHz clock, set Prescaler = 72-1 and Auto-Reload Register (ARR) = 20000-1 to yield a precise 50Hz frequency.
Powering multiple servo motors directly from BluePill 3.3V lines will cause a brownout crash.

title: STM32 internal Temperature Sense Logger
connections: No external sensors required (uses internal STM32 silicon temperature channel)
tips: The internal temp sensor is not designed for precise outdoor readings; it is meant to detect internal MCU thermal overload.
Enable the internal reference voltage (VREFINT) channel to measure and compensate for fluctuations in external VDD supply rails.
